name: Chinese Steamed Pork Buns - 1
recipeInstructions:
1. Combine Hoi sin sauce, oyster sauce, soy sauce and sesame oil. Chop pork and onions finely.
2. Heat vegetable oil in wok or fry pan over high heat. Stir-fry ginger and garlic in the oil for 1 minute. Stir in Hoi sin mixture. Cook and stir for two minutes.
3. Combine 1/2 cup of water and cornstarch. Blend into Hoi sin mixture. Cook until liquid boils. Reduce heat to medium and simmer for 2 minutes. Stir in pork and onions. Remove from heat and let cool completely.
4. In a large bowl, combine flour, baking powder and salt. Cut or rub in shortening until the mixture resembles breadcrumbs. Combine remaining 1/2 cup of water and vinegar. Mix into flour until dough sticks together. Shape dough into a ball. Knead on lightly floured surface for 6-8 times. Cover with plastic wrap and let stand for 20 minutes.
5. Uncover and knead for an additional 4-5 times. Divide dough into 12 equal portions. Shape each portion into a smooth ball.
6. Roll each ball of dough on a lightly floured surface into a circle, 5-6 inches in diameter. Brush the edges lightly with water. Spoon a heaping tablespoon of pork mixture onto the center of each circle. Carefully pinch edges together to seal dough around filling. Bring the two ends of dough over the seam and pinch together.
7. Cut waxed paper into twelve 5-inch squares. Brush one side of paper lightly with oil. Place a bun, seam side down, on each square.
8. Steam buns in single layer on steamer rack over boiling water. Cover and steam until done for about 20 minutes.
Yield: 1 dozen Barbecued Pork Buns
